option_line: bffi_mpSteal9b:"Incredible. What would it take to persuade you to hold a tasting? Menes?"
original: Incredible. What would it take to persuade you to hold a tasting? Menes?

option_line: bffi_mpSteal9c:"Fascinating. Do you think Leonis shot anyone with this, Menes?"
original: Fascinating. Do you think Leonis shot anyone with this, Menes?

"Ah? Oh yes, I cannot deny the temptation to open it for some appropriate event, given an appropriately appreciative audience- my dear cousin Horus for instance; I considered his marriage to Asherah, but I thought- no, not yet. I wonder at times if the delight of it is held by the anticipation, the thought..."

"Oh? Oh, well, I do have a story for you! During negotiations with the buyer I thought, let us have a firearms expert examine this, and wouldn't you know that I happened to be hosting a wealthy financier who's done some work with Tri-Tachyon - and say what you will for the Company, they are good at what they do - and she happened to have a brother with an interest in a firm..."

You both find Bornanew, still on his knees, with one hand stretched out to touch the glove - one finger upon it - lips still moving, eyes still blankly huge as if overcome by religious mania.

Menes gives out a wordless cry of alarm, and begins lifting his cuff to call security.

Your off-hand instinctively curls around a control interface for one of the clever little thieves-tools you have hidden about your uniform.

Menes hesitates as you speak, then barks a codeword into his comms.

You freeze, understanding what this sort of protocol looks like. The lights flare into harsh illumination and a trio of gun turrets pop from subtle cupolas - one each tracks instantly to you and Bornanew while the third spins in a slow covering pattern.

The human guards arrive a moment later, uniforms bright-white, boots squeaking on the floor. Menes breathes heavily, watching the security net tighten.

Bornanew, meanwhile, has not moved but to turn his head toward you and Menes.
"Menes, Yaribay sir. I pray your forgiveness. I- I was overcome by... I had to..." Bornanew trails off, pathetically.

It takes a second, but Menes starts to laugh.

And laugh, and laugh deep in his belly, almost a shout. He suddenly throws an arm over your shoulder, putting you off balance, as he struggles to contain himself.
